title: Automatic Deposition of DAN Matrix using a TM Sprayer for MALDI Analysis of Lipids
author: Elizabeth Neumann
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bt8hnrt6

steps.instruction: Remove slides from freezer and place in desiccator for 00:30:00 .
Scan for autofluorescence on Zeiss Axio Scanner.
Change nitrogen to 6 psi and turn on sprayer.
Open software and set nozzle temperature to 40 °C
Change LC solvent to A1 to 100% acetonitrile at 0.05 mL/min.
“Load” sample loop with 5 mL acetonitrile.
Switch sprayer to “Inject” and spray for 2 minutes.
“Load” 6 mL of matrix solution into sprayer loop.
Switch sprayer to “Inject” and spray for ~1 minute. Use slide to check that matrix is flowing.
Tape slide(s) onto 75 °C heated block on top of stage and adjust software scanning area.
Set method in software: 1350 mm/min nozzle velocity 1.5 mm track spacing 0.05 mL/min flow rate CC Pattern 2 L/min flow rate 5 passes 40mm nozzle height No drying time
Save method and make sure it is highlighted.
Under Cycle, click “Start,” then click “Continue.”
When finished, remove slide and place in slide box.
Set TM sprayer temperature to 30 °C .
Switch A1 solvent line to 100% ACN and set flow rate to 1 mL/min.
“Load” loop with 20 mL acetonitrile and “Inject” loop for 00:10:00 .
Spray tip of nozzle with 5 mL of MEOH.
Log the pressure at your flow rate post cleaning. It must be within 3-4 psi of the starting pressure; if it is not, the cleaning procedure must be repeated.
Change the HLPC flow rate to 0.05 mL/min.
Spray nozzle with methanol.
Remove the foil and wipe stage down with methanol.
Replace wypall and bench diapers.
Place all syringes in the biohazard container and empty the solvent waste.
Shut down the software and turn off the TM Sprayer.
Shut the nitrogen off.
